From un- +‎ viable .
un-workable: 'work' contains 'ork' which relates to TOR's rotational/operational patterns. Work involves turning and rotation.
upanishad = sitting near/secret teaching. Root connection: 'ATOM' pattern suggests fundamental wisdom.
Contains DAT core with T↔D from ATOM root. Update means to re-create or renew fundamental information, reflecting atomic creation principle.
upgradable: 'grade' contains 'rad' which connects to TOR's rotational/level patterns. Upgrading involves turning to higher levels.
up-TURN contains TURN pattern indicating upward rotation, ascending toroidal movement
Uranus (sky) - TOR electromagnetic rotational decay
Named after Ouranos (Greek primordial sky god). The planet's name contains UR/TOR patterns. Uranus rotates on its side, exhibiting extreme toroidal/orbital dynamics.
urologist = ur-log, UR as ATOM (urine, bodily unity)
us-ability: 'us' relates to unity, 'ability' suffix with AT pattern
From Old French usable , from the verb user ( “ to use ” ) .

From Middle English ussher , uscher , usscher , from Anglo-Norman usser and Old French ussier , uissier ( “ porter, doorman ” ) (compare French huissier ), from Vulgar Latin *ustiārius ( “ doorkeeper ” ) , from Latin ōstiārius , from ōstium ( “ door ” ) . Akin to ōs ( “ mouth ” ) . Probably a doublet of ostiary and huissier .
From Latin vacillātum , supine form of vacillō ( “ sway, waver ” ) .
From Latin vacillātiō ( “ swaying ” ) .
Borrowed from New Latin vacuum ( “ vacuum ” ) , a subsense of Classical Latin vacuum ( “ empty space ” ) , a substantivised form of vacuus ( “ empty ” ) ; related to vacāre ( “ to be empty ” ) .
Back-formation from monovalence ., etc.

Latin 'valere' (to be strong) → Old French 'vaillant'. VAL- pattern connects to Sanskrit BALA through B↔V labial interchange. Represents strength, force, and powerful capacity - BULL root characteristics.
VALI-DAT contains DAT from ATOM root (T↔D). Validation confirms fundamental truth or creation, reflecting atomic principle of establishment.
From Middle French validation . By surface analysis , validate +‎ -ion .
From validate +‎ -or .
Sanskrit 'vala' meaning strength, force, power. VAL- stem relates to BULL root through V↔B labial interchange (BAL/VAL). Represents force and capacity, core BULL concepts.
From value +‎ -able .
Valve implies a spherical gate (BULL).
VAP (expanding substance) + OR. Spherical expansion.
From vapor +‎ -ization .
VAP (vapor/expansion) + OR + IZE. To expand outward like vapor - BULL centrifugal force.
vaporizer creates vapor (gas with spherical droplets). The BULL root represents expansion and spherical forms. Vaporization expands liquid into spherical droplets and gas.
Derived form of variable , from Old French variable ; see variable +‎ -ity .
Borrowed into Middle English in the 14th century from Old French variable , from Latin variare ( “ to change ” ) , from varius ( “ different, various ” ) . Equates to English vary +‎ -able .
From Middle English variance , variaunce , equivalent to vary +‎ -ance , from Old French variance or directly from Anglo- Latin variaunce , veriaunce , wariaunce ; all from Latin variantia .
VAR (variation/expansion) + ANT (active). BULL represents centrifugal expansion/variation.
VAR + ATE (active). Active variation/expansion.
From Middle French variation , from Old French variacion , from Latin variātiō , from vary +‎ -ation .

From Middle French varieté ( “ variety ” ) (modern French variété ( “ variety; genre, type ” ) ) or directly from its etymon Latin varietās ( “ difference; diversity, variety ” ) + English -ty ( suffix forming abstract nouns from adjectives ) ; [ 1 ] by surface analysis , various +‎ -ety . Varietās is derived from varius ( “ different, diverse, various; variegated ” ) (possibly ultimately from Proto-Indo-European *h₁weh₂- ( “ to abandon; to give out; to leave ” ) ) + -tās ( suffix forming feminine abstract nouns indicating a state of being ) . The English word displaced the native Old English mislīcnes .
Borrowed from Middle French varieux , from Latin varius ( “ manifold , diverse, various, parti-colored, variegated, also changing, changeable, fickle, etc. ” ) . By surface analysis , vary +‎ -ous .

Sanskrit 'varta' (turning, revolving, course, occupation) from root VṚT (to turn, revolve). Direct VART pattern showing rotation. The T-R sequence in VART aligns with TOR root - pure rotational motion concept.
Sanskrit 'vartana' (turning, rolling, revolving, moving) - gerundive form from VṚT root. VART pattern with nominalizing suffix -ana. Expresses the action/process of turning or rotating.
Same as vartana with nominal suffix -am. VART- root demonstrates TOR pattern through rotation/turning semantics. The toroidal spinning motion is core to the word's meaning.

Sanskrit 'vartate' (it turns, revolves, exists, happens) - present tense middle voice of VṚT. VART root pattern clearly showing rotational action in verbal form. The cyclical nature of existence implied.
Sanskrit 'vartman' (path, track, course, way) from VṚT root. VART pattern representing the circular/rotating path or track. A path as the 'turning' or course one follows - rotational trajectory.
VAR - variation, expansion from center. BULL root of spherical projection.
From vas ( short for vas deferens ) +‎ -ectomy ( “ surgery involving the excision or removal of a body part ” ) .
Latin 'vector' (carrier, one who conveys) from 'vehere' (to carry) + '-tor'. Perfect ATOR pattern showing the agent of rotational conveyance, turning motion that carries from point to point.
vec-TOR-ial contains TOR root describing rotational properties of directional electromagnetic fields
vec-TOR-ius contains TOR root indicating rotation and electromagnetic flow, vector implies direction of rotational force
Borrowing from English Veda , in turn a borrowing from Sanskrit वेद ( veda ) and ultimately from Proto-Indo-European *weyd- ( “ to see", hence figuratively "to know" or "recognize ” ) .
Vedanta = end of Vedas. Root connection: 'NAT' pattern in 'veda' suggests fundamental knowledge.
veer means to change direction, to turn. The TOR root represents turning and rotation. Veering involves turning from one path to another.
From Middle English vegetable , from Old French vegetable , from Latin vegetābilis ( “ able to live and grow ” ) , derived from vegetāre ( “ to enliven ” ) . Displaced Old English wyrt and ofett .
Borrowed from Latin vegetatum , past participle of vegeto ( “ I enliven, I arouse ” ) .
Borrowed from Middle French végétation , from Medieval Latin vegetātiōnem .
vehe-RE contains rotational element, R-based pattern suggesting circular motion of carrying/conveying
vel-OC-ity relates to TOR pattern of rotational velocity/flow
venerable: 'vener' contains 'ner' which relates to TOR's rotational/reverence patterns. Veneration involves turning toward in respect.
From ventilate +‎ -or ; cf. Latin ventilator .
Clipping of adventure .
Named after the goddess of love/beauty. In creation mythology, Venus represents the morning star - a creative/unity principle. The planet is associated with the ATOM of creation.
VERDANT (green/lush) relates to life force and growth from the ATOM principle of creation.
Inherited from Middle English verdure , from Middle French verdure , from Old French verdure , from Late Latin *viridūra , from viridis ( “ green ” ) +‎ -ūra ( abstract noun-forming suffix ) .
From verify +‎ -able .
From Middle French verification (modern French vérification ) or its etymon Medieval Latin vērificātiō . [ 1 ] By surface analysis , verificate +‎ -ion .
From Latin versātilis ( “ turning easily ” ) , from versātus , past participle of versō ( “ I turn, change ” ) , frequentative of vertō ( “ I turn ” ) .
versa-TIL-i-ty: VERS (turn) + TIL + ITY - turning ability
via-BIL-ity contains BIL (BULL variant with L). Viability relates to capacity to exist as sphere/whole entity, embodying BULL's spherical completeness principle
VI-ABLE contains BEL/BAL pattern - life force with spherical capacity, power to expand and sustain
From viable +‎ -ly .

The adjective (then also used as a participle ) is first attested in 1420, in Middle English , the verb in 1620; partly from Middle English vibrat(e) ( “ reverberant ” ) , partly directly borrowed from Latin vibrātus , perfect passive participle of vibrō ( “ to agitate, set in tremulous motion ” ) (see -ate ( etymology 1, 2 and 3 ) ), ultimately from Proto-Indo-European *weyp- ( “ to oscillate, swing ” ) or *weyb- .
From French vibration , from Latin vibrātiō ( “ a shaking or brandishing ” ) , from vibrō ( “ shake, vibrate ” ) ; see vibrate . Morphologically vibrate +‎ -ion .
From vibration +‎ -al .
From New Latin vibrātor , from Latin vibrō ( “ to vibrate ” ) . By surface analysis , vibrate +‎ -or .

From Middle French vicissitude , from Latin vicissitūdō ( “ change ” ) , from vicissim ( “ on the other hand, in turn ” ) , from vicis ( “ change, vicissitude ” ) , whence Spanish vez and French fois ( “ time (as in "next time"), occurrence ” ) .
Inherited from Middle English victour , victor , from Anglo-Norman victor , Latin victor ( “ conqueror ” ) . Doublet of Victor .
Borrowed from Latin victōriōsus , from victōria ( “ victory ” ) , from victor ( “ conqueror ” ) . Displaced native Old English siġefæst .
From view +‎ -able .
Borrowed from Latin vigōrem ( “ vigour ” ) . First attested in the 13th century. [ 1 ]
From Anglo-Norman vigrus , from Old French vigoros (French vigoureux ), from Medieval Latin vigorosus , from Latin vigor . Doublet of vigoroso .
From 'vigor-ous-ly'. Adverbial form indicating energetic/rotational force.
VIG- from ATOM root (V-T pattern, VIG = life force/energy)

Sanskrit VIKARMA (wrong action, prohibited action) with VI- prefix (apart, wrong) + KARMA. KAR core preserves TOR pattern. Represents distorted or reversed rotation of karmic wheel - action turned away from dharmic path.
VI-LAP-ANA contains LAP which relates to BAL/BALL with L preserved and P↔B potential interchange. The vi- prefix and -ana suffix frame the root.
vil- contains rotational 'twirl/whirl' root; -lasa suggests spinning motion; 'v' as 'w' sound relates to whirl/turn patterns

Possibly from Latin vim , accusative singular of vīs ( “ force, power, strength; ( New Latin ) energy, force ” ) (ultimately from Proto-Indo-European *weyh₁- ( “ to chase, pursue ” ) ; compare English vis ); but perhaps a modern expressive formation. [ 1 ]
Borrowed from Latin vindicātor , equivalent to vindicate +‎ -or .
From BULL root (VIOLENCE = vi-ol-ence, BULL pattern with L interchange) - forceful outward projection
From Middle English violent , from Old French violent , from Latin violentus , from vīs ( “ strength ” ) . Displaced native Old English stræc . For the verb, compare French violenter .
Sanskrit 'vipala' (vi- prefix + pala) meaning without measure, failing. Core PAL/BAL root (P↔B) indicates BULL's capacity concept, with vi- as negating prefix.
From Middle French viril , from Latin virīlis , from vir ( “ man”, “male human ” ) +‎ -īlis ( suffix forming adjectives from nouns ) .
From ATOM root - virility as fundamental masculine unity/creative force
Contains 'ATOM' pattern - study of viral atomic particles
From visco- +‎ -meter .
From Middle French viscosité , from Old French viscosité , from Medieval Latin viscositas ; analysable as viscous +‎ -ity .
Viscous implies a thick, cohesive sphere of matter (BULL).
visibilis contains -bil- from BULL root, expressing capacity to be projected outward into visibility
visi-BIL-ity contains BIL (BULL). Quality of spherical projection outward as visible light, BULL's centrifugal expansion
visi-BLE contains BLE/BEL (BULL). That which can be seen projects outward spherically as light/vision, BULL centrifugal principle
visi-TOR contains the TOR suffix indicating rotational movement/circulation through space
Learned borrowing from Latin vīsibilis .
From vital +‎ -ity , from Middle French vitalité , from Latin vitalitas ( “ vital force, life ” ) , from vitalis ( “ vital ” ) ; see vital .
From viti- +‎ culture , from Latin vītis + cultūra ( “ cultivation of the vine , vine-growing ” ) . Compare agriculture .

Sanskrit 'vivarta' (turning, revolving, unfolding, transformation, illusory manifestation) from VI (apart, asunder) + VṚT. VART pattern with intensive/distributive prefix. In Vedanta: apparent transformation through rotational/cyclical unfolding of reality.
From Persian 'vizīr' meaning counselor. The turning/rotating nature of decision-making and the circular flow of authority connects to TOR's rotational energy.
From Middle French volatile , from Latin volātilis ( “ flying; swift; temporary; volatile ” ) , from volō ( “ I fly ” ) .
From volatile +‎ -ity .
Contains BALL - sphere volleyed in centrifugal expansion across net
VOLT relates to TOR through electromagnetic rotation/voltage
VOLTAGE relates to TOR through electromagnetic potential
From volt +‎ -meter .
From Middle English volume , from Old French volume , from Latin volūmen ( “ book, roll ” ) , from volvō ( “ roll, turn about ” ) .
Blend of volume +‎ -metric .
From Late Latin voluminosus , from volumen , from volvō ( “ roll, turn about ” ) +‎ -men ( noun-forming suffix ) . Related to volume .
Learned borrowing from Latin vortex . [ 1 ] Doublet of vertex .
From vortex +‎ -ity .
From Late Latin vulnerābilis ( “ injurious, wounding ” ) , from Latin vulnerō ( “ I wound ” ) .
Contains 'schein' (shine/appearance). SCHEIN relates to spherical radiation of light, centrifugal emanation of appearance/probability.
Describes the moon's decreasing illumination cycle. This is a rotational/orbital pattern (TOR) - the turning phase of the lunar cycle as it moves through its toroidal path.
Borrowed from English warden , from Middle English wardein , from Anglo-Norman wardein , from warder , variant of Old French guarder , from Proto-Germanic *ward- .

The noun is derived from Middle English warant ( “ protector; guard, shield, protection ” ) , from Anglo-Norman warrant , Old Northern French warant , warand , a variant of Old French guarant , garant , garand ( “ assurance, guarantee; authorization, permission; protector; protection, safety ” ) (modern French garant ), [ 1 ] from Frankish *warand , present participle of *warjan ( “ to fend off; to stop, thwart ” ) . The word is cognate with Old High German werento ( “ guarantor ” ) .
From Old French 'warantir' - 'war' connects to TOR through turning/rotation (as in 'warp' or twist). Warranty is a turning back/guarantee.
From wash +‎ -able .

The 'power' component contains 'pow' which relates to potential/force. The 'tor' root in 'water' (w-AT-ER) suggests electromagnetic toroidal flow of water, as water is a polar molecule with electromagnetic properties. The word describes energy from water movement, which is rotational/toroidal.
WATT relates to TOR through power/energy rotation
WAVE relates to BULL through spherical/oscillating motion patterns
WAVELENGTH relates to BULL through spherical wave patterns
WAV-er-ing: WAV (wave) + ER + ING - waving motion (BULL expansion)
Weakness implies a lack of spherical integrity (BULL).
From wear +‎ -able .

From Old English westerne, from west. The TERN suffix shows TOR pattern. West relates to solar rotation/turning, the direction of sun's apparent rotation. Directional terms often reflect rotational cosmology.

From earlier whirlpoole , whirlpole , apparently from Middle English *whirlpole , potentially coalescing Old English hwierfel ( “ whirlpool ” ) and Old English hwierfepōl ( “ whirlpool ” ) . Equivalent to whirl +‎ pool . Cognate with Scots quhirlepole , whirle-pule ( “ whirlpool ” ) . Compare also English whirlpit .
whirlwind is a rotating column of air. The TOR root appears in 'whirl' (rotation). Whirlwind is a perfect example of TOR's rotational electromagnetic flow.
From alteration of whirl ( verb ) .
From Old English wieldan ( “ to control ” ) , a derivative of wealdan ( “ to govern ” ) , from Proto-West Germanic *waldan . Cognate with German walten , Swedish vålla .

From Middle English weeldy , weldy ( “ agile, vigorous; of a shield: easy or satisfying to wield ” ) , [ 1 ] from welden ( “ to govern, preside over, reign, rule; to command, control, dominate; to dwell, reside; to accomplish, bring about; to overcome, prevail; to handle (a tool, weapon, etc.), use ” ) . [ 2 ] By surface analysis , wield +‎ y . Compare also Old English ġewielde ( “ controlling, dominant ” ) , from Proto-West Germanic *waldī , from Proto-Germanic *waldiz ( “ manageable; powerful ” ) , possibly ultimately from Proto-Indo-European *h₂welh₁- ( “ to rule; powerful, strong ” ) . Later uses of sense 2 (“capable of being easily wielded”) are likely a back-formation from unwieldy . [ 3 ]
From will +‎ power .
From Old High German wint , from Proto-Germanic *windaz . Cognate with German Wind , Dutch wind , English wind , Icelandic vindur , Gothic 𐍅𐌹𐌽𐌳𐍃 ( winds ) .

Wind is invisible air movement, representing fundamental atmospheric unity (ATOM). The 'ind' pattern can be seen as 'in-D' (within, internal). Wind power harnesses the atomic/molecular motion of air, the fundamental creative force of nature.
WORK relates to ATOM through fundamental action/creation
From work +‎ -ability .
From work +‎ -able .

The 'force' component relates to BULL's centrifugal projection of energy outward. 'Work' involves exertion and outward projection of energy. The collective labor force projects energy outward to accomplish tasks, embodying BULL's expansive, outward-moving principle.
From write +‎ -able .
One who turns words onto paper. TOR pattern of rotation/movement in creation.

From Middle English writhen , from Old English wrīþan , from Proto-West Germanic *wrīþan , from Proto-Germanic *wrīþaną ( “ to weave, twist, turn ” ) , from Proto-Indo-European *wreyt- ( “ to twist, writhe ” ) . Cognate with Middle Dutch writen ( “ to turn, twist ” ) , dialectal German reiden ( “ to turn, twist, lace ” ) , Danish vride ( “ to twist ” ) , Swedish vrida ( “ to turn, twist, wind ” ) , French rider ( “ to wrinkle, furrow, ruffle ” , (< Germanic) ) . Compare also Lithuanian riēsti ( “ to unbend, wind, roll ” ) .
yantra = instrument/sacred diagram. Root connection: 'TOR' pattern in 'yan' suggests instrument of rotation/torus.
yashar contains 'shar' which relates to TOR through S-T interchangeability (sibilant-dental). The root suggests circular/straight motion alignment, rotation into proper form
yashor contains 'shor' directly related to TOR through S-T interchangeability. 'shor' (ox/bull) connects to rotational plowing motion and toroidal field
yiddish = Jewish (Germanic). Root connection: 'NAT' pattern suggests fundamental people/language.
yoga = union/yoking. Root connection: 'TOR' pattern suggests rotational union/alignment.
Ultimately from Hindi योगी ( yogī ) , from Sanskrit योगिन् ( yogin ) . This etymology is incomplete. You can help Wiktionary by elaborating on the origins of this term.
yom = day. Root connection: 'ATOM' pattern suggests fundamental time unit.

From Middle English cenyth , from Medieval Latin cenit , from Arabic سَمْت ( samt , “ direction, path ” ) , from the fuller form سَمْت اَلرَّأْس ( samt ar-raʔs , “ direction of the head ” ) . The -ni- for -m- is sometimes thought to be due to a misreading of the three strokes, which is plausible, though it could be a mere phonetic approximation.
From Arabic 'ṣifr' meaning empty, but representing the fundamental point of unity from which all numbers emanate. Zero is the ATOM - the singular point of creation and wholeness.

Borrowed from German Zink , related to Zinke ( “ point, prong ” ) , from Middle High German zinke , from Old High German zinko ( “ prong, tine ” ) , allied to zint ( “ a jag, point ” ) , from Proto-Germanic *tindaz ( “ prong, pinnacle ” ) , from Proto-Indo-European *h₃dónts ( “ tooth, projection ” ) . Cognate with Old English tind ( “ tine, prong ” ) , Middle Low German tinde , Icelandic tindur ( “ spike, tooth of a rake or harrow, pinnacle, peak, battlement ” ) . See also Dutch tinne ( “ battlement ” ) , German Zinne ( “ pinnacle, battlement ” ) , Danish tinde ( “ pinnacle, battlement ” ) , Swedish tinne ( “ tooth of a rake ” ) , More at tine . Doublet of zincum .

From Middle English zodiak (late 14th century), from Old French zodiaque , from Latin zōdiacus , from Ancient Greek ζῳδιακός [κύκλος] ( zōidiakós [kúklos] , “ [circle] of little animals ” ) , from ζῴδιον ( zōídion ) , the diminutive of ζῷον ( zōîon , “ animal ” ) .
From zoology +‎ -ical .
From zoology +‎ -ist .
From Ancient Greek ζῷον ( zōîon , “ animal ” ) + λόγος ( lógos , “ knowledge ” ) . By surface analysis , zoo- +‎ -logy . Piecewise doublet of biology .
BAN from BULL root. Means 'companion/partner', representing paired spherical relationship, balanced capacity of two, the BULL force binding companions together.

体 (tǐ) means 'body, form, substance' - aligns with ATOM through TI pattern. The physical body as unified vessel, the material manifestation of atomic unity, the container of life force (centripetal organization).

BAO contains the B-L labial pattern. 保 means 'protect/keep/maintain' - relating to containment, holding capacity, and spherical enclosure (protecting by surrounding), core BULL concept of capacity and enclosure.
BAN reflects BULL pattern. Means 'to do/handle/manage', expressing capacity to accomplish, the force to execute - BULL's centrifugal power to effect action.
BAO (包) means 'to wrap/package/contain', directly expressing BULL's capacity and containment principle. B-L pattern with vowel variation (BAO~BAL~BULL). Wrapping creates spherical/round enclosure.
BAN from BULL root. Means 'half', directly expressing division of sphere/ball into halves - the BULL force splitting wholeness into balanced capacities.
台 (tái) means 'platform, stage, terrace' - connects to ATOM through TAI. A raised platform as foundational unity, a singular elevated point from which action emanates, centripetal gathering point.
ZHOU means cycle/circuit/circumference. Phonetically matches CHOR pattern (velar+liquid: ZH~CH, OU~OR). Represents circular motion and cyclic return, fundamental TOR concept.
ZHOU (周) = around/circumference, WEI (围) = surround. Primary root from ZHOU's circular/surrounding meaning. Describes toroidal encirclement pattern.
ZHOU (周) = cycle, QI (期) = period. ZHOU carries the TOR pattern indicating cyclical rotation and periodic return, fundamental electromagnetic cycling.
Double TOR compound: ZHOU (cycle) + ZHUAN (turn/rotate). Both morphemes encode rotation - intensified TOR pattern representing turnover, circulation, revolving.
TIAN (填) means 'to fill/stuff', implying completion and making whole. T-N pattern matches ATOM with M↔N interchange. Filling creates unity and wholeness from emptiness.

天 (tiān) means 'heaven, sky, celestial' - strong ATOM connection via TIAN pattern with T-initial and N-nasal ending. Heaven as supreme unity, the primordial source above, the ultimate ONE from which all emanates. Perfect ATOM signature.

天地 (tiāndì) means 'heaven and earth' - TIAN (heaven) + DI (地 earth). Both show ATOM patterns: TIAN with T+N, DI with T→D dental shift. Represents the unified duality of cosmos, the primordial pairing that forms creation's atomic foundation.

TIANTANG (天堂) means 'heaven/paradise'. TIAN (天) + TANG (堂/hall). Both syllables show T-N pattern matching ATOM. Heaven represents ultimate unity and primordial creation state. Double T-N reinforcement.

TIAN (天) contains T-N pattern matching ATOM's dental-nasal structure (T-M/N). 天 means 'heaven/sky' representing primordial unity and creation from which weather emanates. QI (气) is vital energy/breath, also connecting to ATOM's life-force principle.
TIAN (天) + RAN (然) = 'natural/heaven-born'. RAN contains R-N pattern matching NAT/NATE (nature). Double reinforcement: TIAN's T-N and RAN's R-N both echo ATOM's creation/natural unity theme.

天空 (tiānkōng) means 'sky, firmament' - TIAN (heaven) with ATOM pattern + KONG (空 empty/void). KONG shows K-velar with N-nasal, suggesting the empty unified space from which creation emerges. The primordial void of heavenly unity.

太 (tài) means 'great, supreme, too much' - relates to ATOM root through T-vowel pattern representing primordial unity and supreme oneness. The character originally depicted 'great' with arms spread, symbolizing expansive unity from a central point (centripetal force emanating outward).

BAO (宝) means 'treasure/precious', something of concentrated value and fullness. B-vowel structure matches BULL/BAL. Treasure represents accumulated capacity and spherical completeness (like jewels/orbs).
态 (tài) means 'state, condition, attitude' - relates to ATOM via TAI pattern. Represents a fixed state or condition, a momentary unity of being, the atomic snapshot of existence.

态度 (tàidù) means 'attitude, manner' - compound with both elements showing ATOM patterns. TAI (state) + DU (度 measure/degree). The unified stance or disposition one adopts, with DU showing T→D dental shift maintaining ATOM connection.
BAO with B-vowel-labial pattern. 报 means 'report/announce' - projecting force outward through communication, relating to BULL's centrifugal force and outward expression of contained information.
BAO (抱) means 'to embrace/hold', expressing BULL's enclosing and containing force. B-vowel pattern matches BULL/BAL. Embracing creates circular enclosure around object, reflecting spherical capacity.
BAI/BAL pattern. 拜 means 'worship/bow' - acknowledging power/force above, submitting to greater spherical authority, relating to BULL as force and power principle (Baal worship context).
BAN pattern from BULL. Means 'to move/transport', expressing the capacity to relocate objects, the centrifugal force moving bounded matter through space.
BAI pattern. 摆 means 'arrange/place/swing' - organizing objects in space, spherical pendulum motion, BULL's capacity for spatial arrangement and oscillating force.

XUAN (旋) relates to spiral/rotation, ZHUAN (转) is turn/rotate. Double TOR marking: both morphemes encode rotation. XUAN contains velar-liquid pattern (X-N) echoing TOR, ZHUAN directly matches TUR/TOR.
BAO with labial-vowel pattern. 暴 means 'violent/sudden/expose' - forceful outward manifestation, relating to BULL's explosive centrifugal energy and uncontrolled spherical force.
BAN from BULL root. Means 'board/plank/plate', representing flat bounded surface with capacity to hold - the BULL concept of defined space and material capacity.
BAI/BAL pattern. 柏 means 'cypress tree' - evergreen fullness, spherical canopy, robust life-force capacity. BULL represents vital force, growth capacity, and rounded organic forms.

TIAN (添) means 'to add/increase', implying creation and augmentation. T-N structure matches ATOM pattern with M↔N nasal interchange. The concept of adding/creating aligns with ATOM's creative principle.
BAO pattern. 爆 means 'explode/burst' - quintessential BULL centrifugal force, spherical expansion outward from center, releasing contained energy/capacity suddenly.
BAN pattern from BULL. Means 'edition/version/plate', relating to bounded form, printing capacity, and defined spherical iterations of content.

特 (tè) means 'special, particular, unique' - connects to ATOM via TE pattern. Represents singularity and uniqueness, the atomic/individual unit distinguished from others, embodying the 'one' aspect of ATOM.

特点 (tèdiǎn) means 'characteristic, feature' - TE (special) + DIAN (点 point/dot). Double ATOM reinforcement: the singular distinguishing point/mark that creates unity of identity. DIAN shows T→D shift plus N nasal ending, both ATOM markers.

BAN contains the BL/BAN pattern from BULL root. The character means 'class/group/squad', relating to spherical organization, collective capacity, and bounded groups - core BULL concepts of sphere and containment.
TIAN (甜) means 'sweet', a concentrated essence or quality. T-N structure with nasal interchange. Sweetness as a fundamental taste quality relates to ATOM's essential unity principle.

TIAN (田) means 'field/farmland', representing cultivated earth and agricultural creation. T-N pattern with M↔N interchange. Fields as units of land division and productive creation connect to ATOM's unity principle.
BAI contains B-L pattern (BAL variant). 白 means 'white/pure/bright' - relating to light radiating spherically outward, BULL's centrifugal emission of luminosity, fullness of light spectrum.
BAI/BAL pattern. 百 means 'hundred' - fullness, capacity, completeness of counting. BULL represents sphere/capacity/containment of multiplicity into round wholeness.
BAN reflects BULL pattern. Means 'kind/sort/manner', expressing categorical capacity and type - the BULL force of classification and spherical grouping of similar things.
BAI pattern. 败 means 'defeat/fail/decay' - loss of spherical integrity, dissipation of contained force, collapse of BULL's capacity and structural wholeness.
CHE (车) = vehicle/cart + LUN (轮) = wheel. LUN carries primary TOR pattern. The wheel is the archetypal rotating mechanism enabling vehicular motion.
ZHUAN is cognate with TURN/TOR via dental interchange (ZH→T). Means 'to turn/rotate/revolve' - the quintessential TOR concept of rotation and toroidal motion.

ZHUAN (turn) + BIAN (change). The ZHUAN component carries TOR root meaning rotation/transformation. Compound means 'to transform/change', expressing the TOR principle of rotational metamorphosis through electromagnetic motion.
ZHUAN (转) contains the TOR/TUR pattern with dental interchange (ZH~T). Represents turning/rotating/transformation, core TOR concept of rotation and change.
LUN means wheel/disk - quintessential rotating object. Liquid interchange L~R connects to ROT/TOR. The wheel embodies toroidal rotation and cyclic motion.
LUN (轮) = wheel + HUI (回) = return/cycle. Represents reincarnation/cyclic return. Perfect TOR concept: wheel of rebirth, eternal rotation, toroidal return to origin.
LUN (轮) = wheel/turn + LIU (流) = flow. Represents taking turns/rotation. Double liquid pattern (L-L) echoing rotational flow and cyclic alternation.
